assessments.condition: makeRun seeds hero at FULL resolved maxHP (1e6 sentinel clamped to resolved max, not base maxHP) — i.e. the prior ~66%-HP bug is fixed
assessments.detail: makeRun sets hero.currentHP/currentStamina/currentMana = 1_000_000 while keeping base maxHP/Stamina/Mana on hero.* (warrior 100/50/30, mage 70/30/60). createCombatState computes resolved = resolveHeroStats(run) and seeds heroHP = Math.min(currentHP, resolved.maxHP). Because currentHP is a 1e6 sentinel, the min resolves to the FULL leveled maxHP. I confirmed this at runtime (not just by reading): a probe wiring up makeRun+createCombatState produced heroHP === heroMaxHP === resolved.maxHP for every leveled case — warrior runXP560 -> 157/157 (base 100), runXP2300 -> 219/219, runXP4400 -> 253/253; mage runXP560 -> 127/127 (base 70), etc. Stamina/Mana also seed to full resolved max (warrior sta 50/50, mage mana 60/60). The only case where heroHP equals base is runXP=0 (level 0, no bonus), which is correct.
assessments.evidence: deck-battle-sim.test.ts:151 currentHP:1_000_000; CombatState.ts:228 heroHP: Math.min(run.hero.currentHP, resolved.maxHP); runtime probe: 'warrior xp=560 :: resolved.maxHP=157 heroHP=157 heroMaxHP=157 ... base=100'.
assessments.impact_on_findings: If this were wrong (seeding base maxHP), survival/HP-cushion findings would be understated by ~36-60% of maxHP and boss losses would be over-reported. It is correct, so HP-cushion and win-rate findings rest on a rested-hero-at-full-HP benchmark — the intended condition.
assessments.recommended_action: None. Optionally document in the report that the benchmark is a rested hero entering each fight at full HP (best case); real runs may enter chip-damaged, so reported cushions are an upper bound.

assessments.condition: Per-combat hero stats (STR/INT/VIT/DEX/maxHP) are correctly derived from runXP via resolveHeroStats + getInRunLevelBonus + getLevel
assessments.detail: Hand-computed and runtime-confirmed for both classes at the 3 requested stages. getLevel(50*1.15^L cumulative): 560->L7, 2300->L14, 4400->L18. getInRunLevelBonus: maxHP=+6/level, VIT=floor(L/2), DEX=floor(L/4), offense axis=floor(L/2) on STR(warrior)/INT(mage); then VIT*5 added to maxHP last. WARRIOR: boss1(560,L7) STR4 VIT3 DEX1 INT0 maxHP=100+42+15=157; boss4(2300,L14) STR8 VIT7 DEX3 maxHP=100+84+35=219; boss7(4400,L18) STR10 VIT9 DEX4 maxHP=100+108+45=253. MAGE: boss1 STR1 VIT3 DEX1 INT3 maxHP=70+42+15=127; boss4 STR1 VIT7 DEX3 INT7 maxHP=189; boss7 STR1 VIT9 DEX4 INT9 maxHP=223. (Warrior base STR is 1 from makeRun line 157; mage offense goes to INT so mage STR stays 1.) Runtime createCombatState produced these exact numbers. Crucially, all 181 decks in decks-full.json carry NO stats profile and NO relics (verified: 0/0/0), so the pure runXP-derived computation is exactly what the sim used — no hidden stat/relic deltas.
assessments.evidence: HeroStatsResolver.ts:172-205 (constants +6/EVERY2/EVERY4) and :253-261 (in-run fold then VIT*5); XPSystem.ts:29-37 getLevel; runtime: 'warrior xp=2300 maxHP=219 STR=8 VIT=7 DEX=3', 'mage xp=4400 maxHP=223 ... INT=9'; decks-full.json: decks-with-stats=0, decks-with-relics=0.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Damage-output and survival findings depend on STR/INT scaling and maxHP; both are correct and match the design curve. No correction needed.
assessments.recommended_action: None for correctness. Minor cosmetic note (see separate assessment) on stage-model approxLevel labels.

assessments.condition: Boss enemy scaling equals floor(baseHP*(1+(normalMult-1)*0.5)) — the boss half-rate fix is right and not double-applied
assessments.detail: Verified every boss matchup's enemyScaledHP/Damage/Defense in results-full.json against the engine formula. The expander passes loopMultiplier=stage.normalMult (NOT the pre-halved stage.bossMult) with isBoss=true, so scaleEnemyForLoop halves exactly once (loopMult = 1+(mult-1)*0.5, then *bossMultiplier=1.0). All 7 bosses match exactly: doom_knight mult1->HP380 (base380, halfMult1.0); iron_golem mult1.1->HP420 (base400, 1.05); lizard_king mult1.2->407; bog_witch mult1.3->471; desert_golem mult1.4->540; infernal_dragon mult1.5->575; boss_iron_golem mult1.6->611 (base470, 1.30). Damage and defense also match floor(base*halfMult) for all 7. The prompt's spot-checks (doom_knight=380, iron_golem~420, boss_iron_golem=611) are confirmed. No double-application of the half-rate.
assessments.evidence: expand-validation-matchups.mjs:111 'enemy: stage.boss, loopMultiplier: stage.normalMult, isBoss: true'; DifficultyScaler.ts:62-65 'if (isBoss) loopMult = 1 + (loopMult-1)*0.5; loopMult *= config.bossMultiplier'; difficulty.json bossMultiplier=1.0; results scan: 'OK boss_iron_golem mult=1.6 HP got=611 exp=611 | DMG got=15 exp=15 | DEF got=5 exp=5 | base(470/12/4) bossMult=1.3'. Normal enemies also verified: 37/37 distinct (enemy,mult) combos match floor(base*mult), 0 mismatches.
assessments.impact_on_findings: If the half-rate were double-applied, deep bosses would be under-scaled (e.g. boss7 would be ~556 not 611) and boss win-rates over-reported. Scaling is correct, so boss-difficulty findings are trustworthy.
assessments.recommended_action: None.

assessments.condition: No matchups have non-empty errors[] or unexpected timeouts in results-full.json
assessments.detail: Scanned all 857 results: 0 have non-empty errors[]; 0 have timeouts>0 (neither boss nor normal). Every matchup resolved to a clean win/death within the 180s sim cap (engine self-fails at 5min deadlock, never hit). enemyType is consistent: 0 normals mislabeled boss and 0 bosses mislabeled normal.
assessments.evidence: results-full.json meta.count=857; scan: 'results with non-empty errors[]: 0', 'results with timeouts>0: 0', 'timeouts on boss matchups: 0 normal: 0'.
assessments.impact_on_findings: No silent failures or partial matchups polluting averages; every winRate/score aggregates over the full intended repeat count (normals 4, bosses 8). Findings are not contaminated by errored runs.
assessments.recommended_action: None.

assessments.condition: stage-model.json approxLevel annotations match the actual getLevel(runXP) the harness uses
assessments.detail: The approxLevel field is off by ~1 at several stages: boss1 runXP560 is labeled approxLevel 6 but getLevel(560)=7 (L7 needs 551 cumulative); boss4 runXP2300 labeled 15 but =14 (L15 needs 2372); boss7 runXP4400 labeled 19 but =18 (L19 needs 4401, so 4400 is one short of L19). This is COSMETIC ONLY: the harness drives hero stats from runXP via getLevel, never from approxLevel — the field is documentation. The actual in-sim stats are correct (verified above). But the annotation could mislead a reader interpreting findings (e.g. thinking boss7 hero is L19 when it is L18, slightly fewer stat points than the label implies).
assessments.evidence: stage-model.json: boss1 approxLevel 6 / runXP 560; boss4 approxLevel 15 / runXP 2300; boss7 approxLevel 19 / runXP 4400. Computed thresholds: reach L7=551, L14=2019, L15=2372, L18=3783, L19=4401. getLevel: 560->7, 2300->14, 4400->18.
assessments.impact_on_findings: No impact on the simulated numbers (runXP is the driver). Risk is only in human interpretation of the report if approxLevel is quoted as the hero level. Note that runXP=4400 is literally 1 XP short of L19 — a brittle round number.
assessments.recommended_action: Correct approxLevel to match getLevel(runXP) in stage-model.json (or relabel as 'approx, off by ~1'), and bump boss7 runXP to >=4401 if L19 is the intended hero strength for that fight.

assessments.condition: Stage/loop coverage of the deck library is complete and representative
assessments.detail: decks-full.json has 181 decks but the loop25 band has ZERO decks authored (distribution: loop2 6, loop5 12, loop8 13, boss1 38, loop15 27, boss2 23, boss3 22, boss4 15, boss5 9, boss6 9, boss7 7). So no normal-enemy battery is exercised at normalMult=1.2 via the loop25 stage tag (the loop25 NORMAL_BATTERY of werewolf/ancient_tree/baby_dragon is never expanded). Those enemies are still hit at other bands, but the 1.2-band normal data point is absent. Also deck counts thin out at deep stages (boss5/6/7 have only 9/9/7 decks), so deep-endgame findings rest on a smaller sample. This is a coverage/representativeness gap, not an input-correctness bug.
assessments.evidence: decks-full.json stage distribution: '{..."loop15":27,...}' with no loop25 key; expand-validation-matchups.mjs:38 defines loop25 battery but no deck carries stage:'loop25'.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Findings about the 1.2 normal band and deep-endgame (boss5-7) are thinner / partly missing. Aggregate balance conclusions are fine for early/mid stages; treat deep-stage and loop25-band claims as lower-confidence.
assessments.recommended_action: Author a few loop25-stage decks (or fold loop25 normals into a neighboring band) and add more boss5-7 decks if deep-endgame conclusions need to be load-bearing in the final report.

assessments.condition: Hero enters with currentDefense=0 and defenseMultiplier matching class (no accidental defense buff)
assessments.detail: makeRun sets currentDefense:0 and defenseMultiplier from class base (warrior 1.0, mage 0.8); createCombatState resets heroDefense to 0 at combat start regardless. So the hero starts each fight with no armor — the rested-but-unarmored benchmark. defenseMultiplier flows through to heroDefenseMultiplier correctly. No relics means no passive armor/stat injection.
assessments.evidence: deck-battle-sim.test.ts:122,157-158 (defenseMultiplier base, currentDefense:0); CombatState.ts:240 'heroDefense: 0', :242 heroDefenseMultiplier: run.hero.defenseMultiplier.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Defensive cards must build their own armor each fight (realistic). No hidden defense inflating survival. Consistent with the full-HP rested benchmark.
assessments.recommended_action: None.

assessments.condition: Stage-model runXP over-states deep-stage hero power (the central confound: if true, 'deep bosses too easy' is an artifact)
assessments.detail: Re-derived achievable cumulative runXP from the live engine constants (src/data/difficulty.json: baseLoopLength 15, loopGrowth schedule [3,2,2,1,1] cap 40, basicTileCombatChance 0.18, eliteChance 0.15, bossEveryNLoops 10) and the XP table (normal 10 / elite 30 / boss 80, getLevel base 50 *1.15). A VANILLA player (basic tiles only, no placed combat) earns LESS than the stage-model (ratio model/achievable 1.08-1.30), but a normal ACTIVE player who places ~4 terrain combat tiles per loop (50% spawn each — the core planning mechanic) earns MORE than the model at every deep stage. The stage-model is therefore conservative-to-neutral, not inflated. Direction of error is the OPPOSITE of the confound: a real engaged player is at least as strong, often stronger, than the sim hero.
assessments.evidence: Cumulative XP and getLevel by loop. Stage-model: L10=560(L7) L20=1150(L10) L30=1700(L12) L40=2300(L14) L50=2900(L16) L60=3600(L17) L70=4400(L18). VANILLA-achievable: L10=431(L5) L20=932(L9) L30=1480(L12) L40=2075(L14) L50=2693(L15) L60=3335(L17) L70=4000(L18) — levels MATCH model at loops 30/60/70, off by 1 at 20/40/50, off by 2 only at loop10. ACTIVE-4-terrain: L10=597(L7) L30=1979(L13) L50=3525(L17) L70=5165(L20) — EXCEEDS model levels at every deep stage.
assessments.impact_on_findings: The 'deep bosses too easy' finding is NOT an artifact of inflated runXP. Hero power in the sim is conservative; a real player who engages the placement mechanic is even stronger, so deep bosses would be EASIER in the live game, not harder. The finding stands and is if anything understated.
assessments.recommended_action: Keep the stage-model runXP as the floor. Optionally add an 'engaged-player' high-XP variant (active/aggressive placement) to bound the upper end and confirm bosses are trivial across the whole realistic XP band, not just the conservative floor.

assessments.condition: In-run leveling contributes enough hero power that XP-curve realism could swing boss outcomes
assessments.detail: Converted runXP to actual hero stats via getInRunLevelBonus + resolveHeroStats (maxHP = base + level*6 + VIT*5 where VIT=floor(level/2); offense STR/INT = floor(level/2)). The in-run curve moves hero maxHP from 157 (loop10) to only 253 (loop70) — ~60% growth across 60 loops — and offense from +3 to +9. This is a SMALL fraction of total hero power, which is dominated by deck/relics/stat-event investments. Even a 2-level swing between achievable estimates changes maxHP by ~17 and offense by ~1, which cannot flip a boss matchup.
assessments.evidence: WARRIOR maxHP/STR by loop — model vs vanilla vs active4: loop10 157/3 vs 140/2 vs 157/3; loop40 219/7 vs 219/7 vs 225/7; loop70 253/9 vs 253/9 vs 270/10. The whole achievable-XP band collapses to a ~17 HP / ~1 STR spread per stage.
assessments.impact_on_findings: XP-curve uncertainty has negligible leverage on boss-difficulty conclusions. Conclusions about deep bosses are robust to any plausible re-estimate of runXP.
assessments.recommended_action: None required. The in-run leveling layer is too weak to be a confound; document that deck quality, not runXP, drives the boss results.

assessments.condition: Boss HP/damage in results-full.json matches the engine's intended half-rate scaling (prior double-half bug fixed)
assessments.detail: Verified every recorded boss enemyScaledHP against the engine formula scaleEnemyForLoop(isBoss): loopMult = 1 + (normalMult-1)*0.5, then *bossMultiplier(1.0), HP = floor(baseHP*loopMult). All 7 deep bosses match exactly — the expander correctly passes stage.normalMult (not the pre-halved stage.bossMult), so the half-rate is applied ONCE. This independently confirms the half-rate-double-application bug is fixed in the data the findings rest on.
assessments.evidence: doom_knight 380==380, iron_golem 420==420, lizard_king 407==407, bog_witch 471==471, desert_golem 540==540, infernal_dragon 575==575, boss_iron_golem 611==611 (all OK). Deepest boss (loop70, normalMult 1.6) is only 611 HP / 15 dmg vs a 253-270 HP hero — barely +30% over its 470 base.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Confirms the boss inputs are correct. The 'too easy' result is driven by the half-rate cap keeping boss HP near base while normal enemies scale at full rate — a genuine balance property, not a setup bug.
assessments.recommended_action: None. This is correct and the confounding bug is verified absent. (Cross-lane note: whether the half-rate boss scaling itself is desirable is a design question, but it is faithfully reproduced.)

assessments.condition: stage-model approxLevel field is consistent with getLevel(runXP)
assessments.detail: The stage-model's documented approxLevel disagrees with getLevel(runXP) by +/-1 at 5 of 12 stages (boss1 stated L6 actual L7; boss3 L13 vs L12; boss4 L15 vs L14; boss6 L18 vs L17; boss7 L19 vs L18). This is purely a documentation/labeling field — the sim harness (makeRun) seeds hero.runXP and derives the real bonus via getInRunLevelBonus(runXP), it never reads approxLevel — so the simulated hero is unaffected. But the mislabel could mislead a reader interpreting the report into thinking the hero is a level higher/lower than it actually is.
assessments.evidence: stage-model.json vs getLevel: boss1 runXP 560 stated approxLevel 6 but getLevel(560)=7; boss7 runXP 4400 stated 19 but getLevel(4400)=18. Harness uses runXP (deck-battle-sim.test.ts makeRun: hero.runXP = m.runXP) not approxLevel.
assessments.impact_on_findings: No impact on simulated outcomes (approxLevel is decorative). Minor risk of misinterpretation in the written report only.
assessments.recommended_action: Recompute approxLevel = getLevel(runXP) in stage-model.json (or drop the field) so documentation matches the curve the sim actually uses.

assessments.condition: runXP held FIXED per stage ignores that XP keeps accruing during the loops a boss is fought across
assessments.detail: Each boss matchup uses a single point-estimate runXP for its loop band, whereas a live player accrues XP continuously. Because the achievable-XP analysis shows the model sits at the conservative end of the realistic band AND the in-run curve has weak leverage on combat (max ~17 HP per 2-level swing), using a fixed point estimate does not bias boss outcomes. The fixed value is a reasonable central/conservative proxy.
assessments.evidence: Across the entire vanilla->active4 achievable band the per-stage maxHP spread is <=17 HP and offense spread is <=1, far below what would flip a 611-HP/15-dmg boss against a 253+ HP hero.
assessments.impact_on_findings: No material effect. Point-estimate runXP is adequate for the difficulty conclusions.
assessments.recommended_action: None required; if precision is wanted later, sweep runXP across the achievable band per stage rather than a single point.

assessments.condition: Resource sustainability — do decks out-spend the +1 stamina/+1 mana per 4.5s regen and the starting pool (warrior 50s/30m, mage 30s/60m), causing stalls that bias findings?
assessments.detail: The FIXED-ORDER engine does NOT stall on resource starvation: when a card is unaffordable it is skipped (deckPointer advances) and only a 500ms RETRY_DELAY is armed when ALL cards are unaffordable. Cheap front cards therefore keep firing regardless of pool depletion. I re-simulated fixed-order affordability with the real regen model over 120s for all 181 decks: even the most resource-heavy decks still played 48 cards (median 63), i.e. ~0.4-0.7 cards/sec — never a true stall. Cross-checking the worst out-spenders against results-full.json, they win 100% in 5-9s.
assessments.evidence: CombatEngine.ts L19-26: REGEN_INTERVAL=4500, STAMINA_REGEN=1, MANA_REGEN=1, RETRY_DELAY=500; L185-189 unaffordable cards are skipped not waited on. results-full.json: 0/857 matchups had any timeout and 0/857 had errors. Worst modeled out-spenders (war-mixed-bruiser-boss6-1, war-pure-physical-boss3-2, deep-endgame-boss7-war-physical) all show avgWR=1.00 at avgTtkMs 5900-9500 playing only 5-7 cards. Long fights (up to 99100ms, 60 cards, 6 reshuffles) still won, proving regen-gated tempo doesn't lose fights.
assessments.impact_on_findings: None negative — confirms front-loading holds and resource model does not bias archetype/enemy findings. The single caveat is the inverse: fights end so fast that resource sustainability is rarely exercised at all (see deck-cycling concern).
assessments.recommended_action: No change needed for sustainability. The decks are correctly front-loaded (cheapest cards first) — verified: only 2/181 decks (cover-1-3, cover-1-4) are mildly back-loaded (front avg 2.0 vs back 1.2).

assessments.condition: Deck cycling — does the full deck actually get tested, or do only the front cards fire (making large late-game decks effectively untested)?
assessments.detail: Because near-100% win rates come with ~6s kills, the majority of decks never fully cycle. In 67% of matchups (578/857) avgCardsPlayed <= deckSize (deck never completes one pass) and 62% never reshuffle (avgReshuffles < 1). This is STAGE-DEPENDENT and inverted vs. realism: early loop2 decks fully cycle (medCards=15, 3 reshuffles, 0% front-only), but late decks barely scratch the front: loop15 88% front-only, boss5 97%, boss6 92%, boss7 89%, with median 6-8 cards played out of 11-15-card decks and 0 median reshuffles. So findings about LATE-GAME archetypes (loop15-boss7) are really findings about each archetype's opening 6-8 cards, not its full 13-15-card deck. The back ~half of every large deck is dead weight that never affects the recorded outcome.
assessments.evidence: results-full.json per-stage: loop2 frontOnly=0%/medResh=3; loop8 55%; boss1 58%; loop15 88%/medResh=0; boss2 86%; boss3 88%; boss4 92%; boss5 97%; boss6 92%; boss7 89%. avgCardsPlayed distribution: median 8, p75 11 vs deck sizes that reach 15. Root cause is enemies dying in ~6s (boss7 decks avgTtk ~6000-7000ms) — an enemy-scaling issue outside this lane — but its CONSEQUENCE is that the late large decks are not realistically exercised.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Late-game archetype rankings and per-card-in-deck conclusions are only valid for the opening cards. Any back-of-deck card (or any claim that a 15-card deck 'works') is essentially unvalidated for loop15-boss7. Comparative archetype findings at late stages may be near-noise because the deciding factor is the front 6 cards, not the archetype's full kit.
assessments.recommended_action: Treat late-stage (loop15+) per-deck/per-card findings as provisional. To genuinely test full late decks, the enemy HP scaling must be raised until late bosses survive long enough to force 1-2 reshuffles (target ttk >> sum(front cooldowns)); OR explicitly scope late-game findings to 'opening sequence' only. Flag this to the enemy-scaling lane as the upstream cause.

assessments.condition: Class fit — warriors mostly stamina, mages mostly mana?
assessments.detail: Class fit is strong. Across all 181 decks only 2 had off-resource-dominant TOTALS, both marginal mage detonator decks (mage-detonator-control-boss7-1: 14 stamina vs 12 mana; boss7-2: 14s vs 13m). On inspection these are NOT real mismatches: the front 7 cards of boss7-1 (the only ones that ever fire) cost 4 stamina / 7 mana — correctly mana-dominant — and the apparent stamina excess is entirely in the never-played back t3 hybrids. Sampled warrior decks are 1s-cost physical/armor/counter cards; sampled mage decks are 1m fire/water/earth cards with hybrid 1s1m/2m t3s pushed to the back.
assessments.evidence: Programmatic scan of cost.stamina vs cost.mana per deck: 2/181 flagged, both mage detonator with deltas of 1-2 points concentrated in back cards. Front-7 resource breakdown of mage-detonator-control-boss7-1 = 4s/7m. Warrior samples (war-pure-physical-loop2-1, war-armor-control-boss4-1) are stamina-only except one trailing 2s1m card; mage samples (pure-burn, pure-poison, mixed-elemental) are mana-led.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Negligible. Class identity is preserved; no warrior deck is secretly a mana deck or vice versa in its played portion.
assessments.recommended_action: Optionally reorder the 2 mage detonator decks so any stamina-heavy t3 sits even further back, but this has no practical effect since those cards never fire.

assessments.condition: No degenerate single-card-carry decks and no unplayable stalls?
assessments.detail: Zero degenerate decks: no deck has any single card id at >=50% of the deck (max duplication is 2x of a cheap card). Distinct-card ratio is high; only 2 decks dip below 60% unique (mage-pure-burn-boss1-2 at 5/9, mage-pure-burn-loop15-2 at 6/11) and both are thematically correct burn-mage fire spam, not degeneracy. All 164 distinct card ids referenced resolve in cards-index.json (0 missing). The 24 cover-* decks are deliberate coverage builds for underused cards, not bias. Null-cost cards (e.g. t1-air) are handled correctly by canAfford (CardResolver.ts L67 'if (!cost) return true') so they never error or block.
assessments.evidence: Scan: DEGENERATE single-card>=50% = 0/181; LOW DISTINCT (<60%) = 2/181 (both pure-burn, intended). MISSING CARD IDS = none. CardResolver.canAfford L65-78. cover-* decks (24) each have notes naming the specific card(s) they exercise.
assessments.impact_on_findings: None — deck composition is realistic and non-degenerate, so per-archetype and per-card findings reflect genuine play patterns.
assessments.recommended_action: No change.

assessments.condition: Deck sizes scale with stage (small early, up to 15 late) and stage coverage is complete?
assessments.detail: Deck sizes scale correctly with stage: loop2 = 5 cards (min/med/max 5/5/5), loop5 5-7, loop8 8-10, boss1 7-11, loop15 9-13, boss2 8-13, boss3 8-14, boss4 10-15, boss5 12-15, boss6 12-15, boss7 13-15. This matches the realistic 'deck grows over the run' progression. HOWEVER there is a coverage GAP: stage-model.json defines a 'loop25' stage (loopCount 25, runXP 1450, approxLevel 12, normalMult 1.2) but decks-full.json contains ZERO decks tagged loop25 — the deck stages present are loop2,loop5,loop8,boss1,loop15,boss2,boss3,boss4,boss5,boss6,boss7. So the loop25 normal-enemy band is entirely unrepresented in the deck library.
assessments.evidence: Per-stage deck-size scan (above). Stages present in decks: 'loop2,loop5,loop8,boss1,loop15,boss3,boss2,boss4,boss5,boss6,boss7' — loop25 absent. stage-model.json L64-73 defines loop25 (normalMult 1.2, runXP 1450).
assessments.impact_on_findings: Minor: the loop25 normal-enemy difficulty band (between boss2 and boss3 progression) has no deck-vs-normal-enemy data, so any claim covering the full progression has a hole there. Boss3 decks (runXP 1700, mult 1.2 via bossMult) partially cover the same power band, so the gap is not catastrophic.
assessments.recommended_action: Either add ~6-10 decks tagged loop25 (mid-late normal band) to close the gap, or explicitly note in the report that the loop25 normal band is inferred from adjacent boss3/loop15 data rather than directly simulated.

assessments.condition: Do any realistic decks produce loss outcomes that could be mistaken for deck problems (vs enemy/balance signals)?
assessments.detail: Only 5/857 matchups had any death and these are the most balance-informative results, but two are worth flagging for interpretation: war-pure-physical-loop2-1 (the canonical 5-card loop2 warrior opener) DIES to 'mush' after 49 cards played, and early-starters-loop2-2 dies to 'mush' after 215 cards played. 'mush' is a NORMAL loop2 enemy with 122 baseHP / 0 defense / 3 damage — nearly 2x the HP of a peer loop2 normal (lost_lizard 68 HP). The decks themselves are realistic (THE starter decks), so these losses are genuine enemy-tuning signals (mush is an HP outlier the low-damage starter can't out-DPS within its stamina-regen tempo over many reshuffles), NOT artifacts of unrealistic decks. The other 3 losses are mage/cover decks vs early bosses.
assessments.evidence: results-full.json losses: war-pure-physical-loop2-1__vs__mush WR=0 deaths=4 cards=49; early-starters-loop2-2__vs__mush WR=0 deaths=4 cards=215; plus 3 boss losses (mage-pure-burn-boss1-2 vs doom_knight, mage-detonator-control-boss3-1 vs lizard_king, cover-2-3 vs iron_golem). enemies.json mush: baseHP 122, baseDefense 0, attack.damage 3 vs lost_lizard baseHP 68.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Positive for deck-realism (decks are valid), but the report must attribute the mush losses to enemy HP tuning, not to the decks. A 215-card grind-then-die is the one place the resource model genuinely bit, and it correctly surfaced as a real signal.
assessments.recommended_action: In the findings, label the mush losses as enemy-tuning outliers (mush 122 HP vs peer ~68) rather than deck failures. This validates that the simulator surfaces real balance problems through realistic decks.

assessments.condition: All 164 cards appear in >=1 simulated deck
assessments.detail: Every card id in cards-index.json (164) appears in at least one deck in decks-full.json (181 decks, 164 distinct cards used), and every one of those decks was actually simulated. Cross-checking results-full.json: 0 cards are absent from a simulated matchup, 0 orphan card ids (used-but-not-indexed), 0 index-but-unused.
assessments.evidence: cards-index ids:164; distinct cards used in decks-full:164; cards NEVER in any deck:0; decks authored but NOT simulated:0 (181/181); cards NOT in any simulated matchup:0. overview.json: nCardsUncovered:0, cardsUncovered:[].
assessments.impact_on_findings: None — raw card coverage claim is sound; no card verdict is based on zero data.
assessments.recommended_action: None for raw coverage. (See depth-of-coverage concern below for the quality caveat.)

assessments.condition: All 26 enemies (19 normals + 7 bosses) were simulated
assessments.detail: enemies-index.json contains 26 enemies (19 type=normal, 7 type=boss). results-full.json simulated all 26 distinct enemy ids; no enemy missed; no simulated enemy id absent from the index. overview.json independently reports nEnemiesCovered:26, enemiesNotCovered:[].
assessments.evidence: enemies-index count:26; distinct enemies simulated:26; enemies NEVER simulated:0; simulated enemy ids not in index:0. Boss list: doom_knight,iron_golem,lizard_king,bog_witch,desert_golem,infernal_dragon,boss_iron_golem.
assessments.impact_on_findings: None — full enemy roster was exercised.
assessments.recommended_action: None.

assessments.condition: Aggregator math (enemy-summary.json / card-appearance.json) is accurate vs raw results-full.json rows
assessments.detail: Recomputed meanWinRate, minWinRate, meanHpCushionOnWin, deathRate, nMatchups, nLossesMatchups for 3 enemies (mush, lost_lizard, doom_knight) and nDecks, nNormalMatchups, meanNormalWinRate, meanNormalCushion, meanBossWinRate for 4 cards (t1-attack, t1-fire, t3-attack-attack-attack, t1-counter) directly from the 857 raw rows. Every recomputed value matched the published summary to 3 decimals. meanWinRate is an unweighted mean across matchups; verified no enemy is summarized across BOTH boss and normal matchup types (0 such enemies), so the differing 4-vs-8 repeat counts never bias an enemy mean. Cushion correctly averages only over win rows (avgHeroHpPctOnWin != null). 0 results carry errors, 0 timeouts.
assessments.evidence: mush recomputed {n:6,meanWR:0.667,minWR:0,meanCushion:0.942,deathRate:0.333,nLoss:2} == summary {nMatchups:6,meanWinRate:0.667,minWinRate:0,meanHpCushionOnWin:0.942,deathRate:0.333,nLossesMatchups:2}. doom_knight recomputed meanWR:0.974,meanCushion:0.592 == summary. t1-attack recomputed {nDecks:63,nNormalMatchups:282,meanNormalWinRate:0.993,meanNormalCushion:0.898,meanBossWinRate:1} == summary exactly. enemies appearing as BOTH boss and normal:0.
assessments.impact_on_findings: None — published aggregates faithfully represent the raw sim rows.
assessments.recommended_action: None. (Minor: deathRate label is ambiguous — for all-0/1 matchups it equals both by-matchup loss-fraction and by-repeat death-fraction; harmless here but worth documenting.)

assessments.condition: Repeats (4 normal / 8 boss) are adequate to average over RNG
assessments.detail: The sim is effectively DETERMINISTIC for win/loss: ALL 857 matchups have winRate of exactly 0 or 1 (734 normal + 123 boss); zero partial winrates anywhere, so RNG never flipped an outcome within any matchup's repeats. Root cause verified in code: (a) NO card effect uses rand() — grep of src/systems/cards/ and CardResolver.ts for rand/Math.random returns nothing; the magic elemMult (SIM_ELEM_RATE) is a deterministic stat-based multiplier, not a roll. (b) The only combat RNG is enemy-side: EnemyAI double-damage (rand()<0.3) requires enemySpecialEffect, and damage variance (0.8+rand()*0.4) requires pattern:'random' — but ALL 26 enemies are pattern:'fixed' with no special-effect, so neither fires. (c) Air-affinity stun (EnemyAffinity.ts: rand()<0.15 normal / 0.30 boss) is live, but the only 2 air enemies (depths_horror, venomous_kobra) won 100% across 77+25 matchups, so stun never mattered. (d) hemlock_vial relic rand()<0.25 only fires if that relic is equipped. Net: repeats add no variance reduction for outcomes — they are redundant but not harmful. The good news: this means findings are NOT RNG-fragile; the bad news is the harness gives a false impression of RNG-averaging, and any genuinely RNG-sensitive content (relic-bearing or future random-pattern enemies) would be under-sampled.
assessments.evidence: normal rep4: all-or-nothing(wr 0 or 1):734 partial:0; boss rep8: all-or-nothing:123 partial:0. grep rand in src/systems/cards/ -> empty; in CardResolver.ts -> empty. enemy patterns:{fixed:26}. air enemies depths_horror(n=77,meanWR=1.0), venomous_kobra(n=25,meanWR=1.0). SharedRNG falls back to Math.random when setActiveRNG unset (sim never sets it), so runs ARE independent — yet outcomes still never varied.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Low for current findings (they are deterministic, not fragile). But the report should NOT claim repeats provide statistical robustness against RNG — for this fixed-enemy roster they do not. If any matchup attaches a random-pattern enemy or a proc relic, 4 repeats would be too few.
assessments.recommended_action: Document that the current roster is deterministic so repeats are a no-op; reallocate the wasted repeat budget toward MORE DECKS per under-sampled enemy. If future content adds random patterns / proc relics, bump repeats to >=20 for those matchups specifically.

assessments.condition: Early base normals (loop1 enemies) adequately sampled; the mush 0.667 finding is robust
assessments.detail: The 6 firstLoop=1 base normals (lost_lizard, giant_spider, mush, forge_slime, toxic_gooze, scorpion) plus pocket_cat are each tested by only ~6 matchups (all at loop2 stage, mult=1.0), whereas mid/late normals fire_elemental and werewolf get 112 each and skeleton 74 — the heavy sampling went to enemies that win 100% trivially, while the one genuinely hard enemy got the least data. The mush 0.667 (= 4 wins / 6 decks) rests on just 6 decks. The 2 losses are DETERMINISTIC (4/4 deaths each, avgTtk=null, no RNG, no timeout): war-pure-physical-loop2-1 (5x tier-1 ['t1-attack','t1-agility','t1-attack','t1-agility','t1-defense']) and early-starters-loop2-2 (starter-physical-haste ['t1-attack','t1-air','t1-agility','t1-defense','t1-counter']). So 0.667 is a real, reproducible signal that weak 5-card tier-1 warrior openers cannot out-DPS mush's 122 HP / 0-defense before dying — NOT an RNG artifact. But with only 6 decks (and only 3 being warrior-physical), one added or removed deck swings the headline number by ~0.167, making the precise 0.667 value statistically thin even though the underlying 'fragile early warrior opener' finding is genuine.
assessments.evidence: matchup count per enemy: lost_lizard/giant_spider/mush/forge_slime/toxic_gooze/scorpion all n=6; fire_elemental n=112, werewolf n=112, skeleton n=74. mush losers: both deaths=4/4, deterministic. mush winRateByArchetype: pure_physical/warrior:0.5, starter-physical/warrior:1, starter-physical-haste/warrior:0, starter-elemental/mage:1, starter-burn/mage:1.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Medium — the QUALITATIVE finding (early tier-1 warrior decks risk death to mush-class loop1 normals) is sound and deterministic. The QUANTITATIVE headline (mush winRate 0.667, ranked 'hardest enemy') is fragile to deck selection because n=6 and the loop2 battery skews warrior-physical-heavy.
assessments.recommended_action: Add 6-10 more loop2 decks spanning the same archetypes against all 6-7 loop1 normals so each early normal has ~15+ data points; re-derive mush's winRate. Keep the qualitative finding; soften the precise 0.667 number until resampled.

assessments.condition: Boss HP/damage/defense scaling in results matches the engine's intended single-half-rate (the prior double-half-rate bug is fixed)
assessments.detail: Verified all 7 boss stages: the expander passes stage.normalMult as loopMultiplier with isBoss:true, and scaleEnemyForLoop applies the half-rate once (1+(mult-1)*0.5) then *bossMultiplier(=1, from difficulty.json). Recomputed expected scaled HP/Dmg/Def for every boss and matched results-full.json exactly. stage-model's bossMult column (1.05,1.1,1.15,1.2,1.25,1.3) equals the computed expMult and is documentation-only (not passed to the scaler), so no double-application.
assessments.evidence: boss2/iron_golem normalMult=1.1 expMult=1.05 -> HP 420(exp 420) Dmg 10 Def 4 OK; boss7/boss_iron_golem normalMult=1.6 expMult=1.30 -> HP 611(exp 611) Dmg 15 Def 5 OK; all 7 stages 'OK'. difficulty.json bossMultiplier:1. expander comment lines 98-103 confirm intentional single-halving.
assessments.impact_on_findings: None — boss difficulty in-sim equals intended live scaling; boss findings are on solid footing.
assessments.recommended_action: None.

assessments.condition: Depth of coverage: every card meaningfully tested (not just present once vs trivial content)
assessments.detail: Raw coverage (every card in >=1 matchup) is satisfied, but depth is thin for many cards: 19 cards appear in EXACTLY 1 deck (some with only 3 normal matchups), 62 cards appear in <=2 decks, and 4 cards (t3-agility-agility-agility, t3-agility-agility-fire, t3-air-defense-fire, t3-counter-earth-water) are NEVER tested against any boss (meanBossWinRate:null) — they only live in loop15/loop25 decks, and stage-model gives loop15/loop25 boss:null, so no boss matchup is generated. Additionally, 2 single-deck cards (t3-earth-earth-fire, t3-agility-earth-fire) have their entire boss verdict (meanBossWinRate:0) decided by a SINGLE boss matchup loss. These per-card means are technically correct but rest on 1-3 data points.
assessments.evidence: cards in exactly 1 deck:19; cards in <=2 decks:62; cards NEVER tested vs a boss:4 (t3-agility-agility-agility in war-pure-physical-loop15-2@loop15 bossMatchups=0; t3-agility-agility-fire in cover-2-2/cover-2-5@loop15 bossMatchups=0; etc.). single-deck cards with meanBossWinRate=0: t3-earth-earth-fire, t3-agility-earth-fire. stage-model loop15/loop25 boss:null.
assessments.impact_on_findings: Medium for the THIN cards — any per-card verdict (especially boss-viability) for the 19 one-deck cards and the 4 never-vs-boss cards is low-confidence and should not be reported as a firm balance conclusion. Aggregate/archetype findings are unaffected.
assessments.recommended_action: Either (a) add boss-stage decks containing the 4 never-vs-boss T3 cards, or (b) flag cards with nDecks<=2 / no-boss-data as 'insufficiently sampled' in card-appearance.json and exclude them from per-card outlier verdicts. Currently the 'covered:true' flag overstates confidence for these.
